title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text: Street food has been a part of urban life for centuries, but it has recently (0) a remarkable revival in cities around the world. Food markets and mobile vendors are now (1) in places where they were once considered outdated. This trend can be (2) to several factors, including a growing interest in authentic culinary experiences. Many people are (3) to the idea of trying dishes that have been passed down through generations. Street food also (4) to those who want a quick, affordable meal without (5) quality or flavour. In some cities, street food vendors have even earned Michelin stars for their exceptional cooking. Food trucks have (6) off in particular, offering everything from gourmet burgers to traditional Asian cuisine. Social media has played a significant role in promoting street food culture, with customers eager to (7) photos of their meals online. (8), what was once seen as cheap fast food is now celebrated as an important part of our culinary heritage.

example.explanation: 'Experienced' is the correct verb to indicate that something has gone through a process or change.

questions.explanation: 'Thriving' indicates that something is developing successfully and prosperously.
'Attributed to' is the correct expression to indicate the cause of something.
'Drawn to' indicates being drawn to something naturally.
'Appeals to' is the correct verb to indicate that something is attractive to someone.
'Sacrificing' indicates giving up something valuable, such as quality.
'Taken off' (with 'off' already present in the text) means to suddenly take off or succeed.
'Share' is the most natural verb for publishing content on social networks.
'Consequently' introduces a consequence of what was mentioned above.
